regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, winds are from the east-northeast at 11 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, providing stable conditions for embarkation and shore excursions. By Day 3, expect a shift to easterly winds at 13 knots accompanied by light drizzle and a 13% rain probability in Miami, which may slightly affect outdoor activities but should not disrupt port operations. Tampa Bay maintains relatively calm conditions but should monitor the slight increase in rain chance moving eastward. Overall, no significant weather disruptions are forecast through this period, but light drizzle may require minor adjustments to shore excursions late in the outlook.
